content.text: Sampling
The EP-133 can sample from the 3.5mm line input, the built-in microphone, or via resampling (OS 2.0). Samples are recorded at 46.875 kHz / 16-bit with a 24-bit ADC for high-quality capture.
Audio Input

Connect your audio source to the orange INPUT jack (3.5mm stereo) on the top panel. The input accepts line-level signals from phones, tablets, turntables (with preamp), synthesizers, and other audio sources.

content.caption: Input port (orange) and Gain knob for level control

content.text: Gain Control

Use the orange GAIN knob (also labeled X) to adjust the input level. Monitor the input meter on the display while adjusting - aim for a strong signal that doesn't clip (peaks around 80% of the meter).

content.title: Optimal Levels
content.text: Set the gain so peaks reach about 80% of the meter. This leaves headroom while minimizing noise floor.
Built-in Microphone

When no external source is connected to the INPUT jack, the EP-133 uses its built-in microphone for sampling. This is convenient for quick captures of environmental sounds, voice, or acoustic instruments.
The built-in microphone is useful for quick captures, but use the line input for higher quality recordings.
Recording a Sample
Follow these steps to record a new sample:

items: Press SAMPLE/CHOP to enter sampling mode
Adjust GAIN to set the input level (watch the meter)
Select a sound slot using number keys + ENTER
Recording starts automatically when audio is detected, or press any pad to start manually
Press RECORD again or any pad to stop recording
The sample is automatically assigned to the selected sound slot

content.caption: SAMPLE/CHOP button enters sampling mode, RECORD arms/stops recording

content.text: Hands-Free Sampling

OS 2.0 introduces hands-free sampling with automatic threshold detection. Hold SHIFT and press SAMPLE to enable this mode. The EP-133 starts recording when audio exceeds the threshold and stops after a period of silence.

content.title: Great for Musicians
content.text: Hands-free sampling is perfect when you need both hands to play an instrument while sampling.
Resampling
Resampling (OS 2.0) lets you record the EP-133's audio output as a new sample. This captures everything: sounds, effects, and fader automation. To resample, hold SHIFT and press SAMPLE/CHOP.
Resampling is useful for: bouncing multiple sounds to free up polyphony, capturing effects processing, creating new sounds from processed material, or making stems of your patterns.
Chopping Samples

Hold SAMPLE/CHOP to enter chop mode. The EP-133 automatically detects transients in the sample and splits it into multiple slices. Each slice is assigned to a different pad, allowing you to trigger individual hits from a longer recording.

items: Automatic transient detection for drum loops and phrases
Adjust slice points manually with +/- and Y knob
Great for chopping drum breaks, vocal phrases, and melodic loops

content.text: Sample Management
Samples can be transferred to and from a computer via USB using the Teenage Engineering EP-133 app. This allows you to backup your samples, organize your library, and import audio files.
